title: Aspidogyne mendoncae
intro: Aspidogyne mendoncae, also known as the Aspidogyne, is a rare and lesser-known species of orchid found in Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as potential antifungal and antiviral effects. The bioactive constituents of Aspidogyne mendoncae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Aspidogyne mendoncae is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
